content.problemStatement:
There is a bus with 30 seats. The seats are numbered from 1 to 30, and the numbering is as depicted in this image.

As can be seen in the image, the bus is divided into two decks - The Lower deck, and the Upper deck, with 15 seats each. And some of the seats come as Single and some as Double. For example, Seats 1 and 2 are Double, whereas Seat 11 is a Single.

You will be given a Seat number, and your job is to classify it as one of these 4 types:


Lower Single
Lower Double
Upper Single
Upper Double

Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single line of input which contains a single integers NNN — the seat number.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the type of seat.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N (1 ≤ N ≤ 30) representing a seat number.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing one of the four strings: "Lower Single", "Lower Double", "Upper Single", or "Upper Double".

sampleTestCases.output:
Lower Double
Upper Single
Upper Double
Lower Single
Lower Double
sampleTestCases.explanation:
Seat 6 → Lower deck, position 6 ≤ 10 → Double → "Lower Double".
Seat 28 → Upper deck, position 13 > 10 → Single → "Upper Single".
Seat 16 → Upper deck, position 1 ≤ 10 → Double → "Upper Double".
Seat 13 → Lower deck, position 13 > 10 → Single → "Lower Single".
Seat 10 → Lower deck, position 10 ≤ 10 → Double → "Lower Double".

sampleTestCases.output:
Lower Double
Lower Single
Upper Single
sampleTestCases.explanation: Seat 1 is in the Lower deck and within the first 10 seats → Double.\nSeat 15 is the last seat of the Lower deck (position 15) → Single.\nSeat 30 is the last seat of the Upper deck (position 15) → Single.

sampleTestCases.output:
Lower Single
Upper Double
Upper Single
Lower Double
sampleTestCases.explanation:
Seat 11 → Lower deck, position 11 > 10 → Single.
Seat 22 → Upper deck, position 7 ≤ 10 → Double.
Seat 27 → Upper deck, position 12 > 10 → Single.
Seat 5 → Lower deck, position 5 ≤ 10 → Double.

solution.approach:
The bus has two decks: seats 1‑15 belong to the Lower deck and seats 16‑30 belong to the Upper deck. Within each deck the first 10 seats are Double seats and the last 5 seats are Single seats. For a given seat number N we:
1. Determine the deck: if N <= 15 → Lower, else Upper.
2. Compute the position inside the deck: pos = N (if Lower) or N - 15 (if Upper).
3. If pos <= 10 the seat is Double, otherwise Single.
4. Print "<Deck> <Type>".
The algorithm runs in O(T) time and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – each test case is processed in constant time.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.

code.Python3:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    t = int(data[0])
    out = []
    idx = 1
    for _ in range(t):
        n = int(data[idx]); idx += 1
        deck = "Lower" if n <= 15 else "Upper"
        pos = n if n <= 15 else n - 15
        typ = "Double" if pos <= 10 else "Single"
        out.append(f"{deck} {typ}")
    sys.stdout.write("\n".join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
